title: NOC 95106 – Labourers in food and beverage processing | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s food and beverage processing sector is a critical pillar of national security and a massive export powerhouse. Labourers in Food and Beverage Processing (NOC 95106) are the essential workforce ensuring the safety, quality, and distribution of Canada’s food supply. From advanced meat processing facilities in the Prairies to high-tech beverage bottling plants in Ontario, these workers are in constant demand. While classified as TEER 5, this role is a top-tier priority for Canadian immigration, offering specialized "Agri-Food" pathways that are among the fastest routes to Permanent Residency for international workers.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category5 (Occupations usually requiring short work demonstration)Major Group95 – Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilitiesSub-Major Group951 – Labourers in processing and manufacturing

duties: Material Handling: Transporting raw food materials, finished products, and packaging materials manually or using forklifts.
Cleaning & Sanitization: Cleaning and disinfecting work areas and machinery to meet strict 2026 Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) standards.
Production Support: Feeding and unloading food processing machines and assisting operators in setting up production lines.
Sorting & Packaging: Manually packing, weighing, and labeling food and beverage products for shipment.
2026 Update: Utilizing digital temperature probes and handheld scanners to log food safety data in real-time.
employment_requirements: Education: Some secondary school education may be required. No formal degree is necessary for entry-level roles.
Training: Short-term on-the-job training is provided.
Physical Fitness: Ability to stand for long periods, perform repetitive tasks, and work in varied temperatures (cold storage or bakery heat).
Language: CLB 4 is usually the minimum requirement for federal pilots like the Agri-Food Pilot.
Safety: Mandatory 2026 WHMIS and Food Safety (Level 1) certification.
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) indicates a "Balanced" market with high turnover, creating over 20,000 job openings due to expansion and retirements. As Canada increases its domestic food processing capacity to combat global inflation, the need for reliable industrial labourers will remain a top national priority.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook for food and beverage labourers is rated as Very Good across Canada due to the essential nature of the industry:
Ontario: High demand. Driven by a massive concentration of food processing hubs in the Greater Golden Horseshoe and Southwestern Ontario.
Alberta & Saskatchewan: Strong prospects. High demand in meat processing and grain-based food manufacturing facilities.
British Columbia: Good prospects. Focused in the Lower Mainland’s diverse beverage and specialty food production sectors.
Atlantic Canada: Critical Need. Specifically in seafood processing plants in Nova Scotia and New Brunswick.
Why Work in Canada as a Food & Beverage Labourer?
Fast-Tracked PR: This is one of the few TEER 5 roles eligible for the Agri-Food Pilot, a dedicated federal pathway to Permanent Residency.
Year-Round Stability: Food and beverage production is an essential service, providing consistent employment regardless of economic shifts.
Strict Safety Standards: Work in highly regulated, clean environments that prioritize worker health and food safety (HACCP).
Growth into Skilled Roles: Easily transition into NOC 92012 (Supervisors) or specialized Process Operators with 1–2 years of experience.
pathways: Agri-Food Pilot (Renewed for 2026): A specialized federal pathway for food processing labourers with 12 months of experience in Canada.
Ontario In-Demand Skills Stream: NOC 95106 is an eligible occupation for those with a valid job offer in Ontario’s food manufacturing sector.
TR to PR Pathway (2026): Labourers in food and beverage are classified as "Essential Workers" and are high-priority candidates for this 33,000-person quota.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-success pathway for those with job offers in the Atlantic seafood or food processing sectors.
